item.title: Exclusive: German semiconductor group Elmos exploring a sale, sources say
item.description: Germany's ​Elmos Semiconductor is exploring a sale as its founders consider exiting the business, according to ‌three people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Goldman Sachs to make small job cuts on performance in April, source says
item.description: Goldman Sachs plans ​to cut a ‌small number of jobs in April of ​staff that have ​underperformed, a source familiar ⁠with the ​matter said.

item.title: Talk of AI disruption, wars, oil and rates dominates M&A conference even as deals soar
item.description: The value of dealmaking is close to all-time highs, even as corporate executives struggle to navigate ‌the Trump administration's tariffs, wars on multiple continents and how artificial intelligence will shape the economy in the years ahead.

item.title: How US regulators are overhauling bank capital rules
item.description: U.S. banking regulators on Thursday unveiled sweeping plans to streamline and ease numerous capital requirements for the nation's largest banks, which could free up billions of dollars for lending, dividends and share buybacks.

item.title: Ecolab nearing deal to acquire KKR's data center cooling company for up to $5 billion, WSJ reports
item.description: Ecolab ​is nearing a ‌deal to acquire data center cooling company ​CoolIT Systems from ​KKR for between $4.5 billion ⁠and $5 billion, the ​Wall Street Journal reported ​on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US fixed 30-year mortgage rate hits three-month high amid Iran war
item.description: The average rate on the popular U.S. 30-year fixed-rate mortgage ​surged to a three-month high ‌this week as war in the Middle East stoked inflation fears, dealing a blow ​to the Trump administration's efforts ​to make housing more affordable.

item.title: Apple fends off bid for new Apple Watch import ban at US trade tribunal
item.description: A U.S. trade tribunal preliminarily ruled that Apple's current Apple Watches do not infringe patents owned by Masimo , rejecting the medical-monitoring company's bid for ​a renewed import ban on the tech giant's smartwatches.

item.title: IMF says prolonged increase in energy prices could boost inflation, lower growth
item.description: The IMF on Thursday said it was closely monitoring developments in the Iran war and the ​resulting disruptions to energy production, warning that prolonged increases in energy ‌prices could boost inflation and lower growth globally.

item.title: Cloud industry group calls for EU interim measure against Broadcom over VMware
item.description: Lobbying group Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe on Thursday urged EU ​antitrust regulators to temporarily stop Broadcom from ending ‌its VMware Cloud Service Provider programme in Europe, ramping up its fight against the U.S. tech company.

item.title: Explainer: How important is food to Unilever?
item.description: Unilever shares were down on Thursday, extending losses from Wednesday after a report the company may spin off its food business, and had held merger talks with Kraft Heinz that have ended.

item.title: US wholesale inventories fall sharply in January
item.description: U.S. wholesale inventories dropped sharply January, a trend that if sustained could ​result in inventory investment being a drag ‌on economic growth in the first quarter.

item.title: US new home sales drop to near 3-1/2-year low in January
item.description: Sales of new U.S. single-family homes fell more than expected in January, hitting their lowest level in nearly 3-1/2 years, likely weighed down by harsh winter weather.

item.title: Big central banks keep options open as traders suspect war will bring rate hikes
item.description: Nearly all major developed market central banks kept rates unchanged this week, but emphasised their readiness to act to curb inflation should the energy shock caused by the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran drive a broader surge in ​prices.

item.title: WInklevosses' Gemini Space Station sued by shareholders over strategy shift, losses, departures
item.description: Gemini ​Space Station and its billionaire founders Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss ‌were sued by shareholders who said they were defrauded about the cryptocurrency exchange's business prospects, and suffered losses as a strategy shift, mounting losses, job ​cuts and executive departures caused the stock price to fall.

item.title: US extends protection of Venezuela-owned Citgo from creditors
item.description: The ​United ‌States has extended ​a ​license that ⁠protects ​Venezuela-owned refiner ​Citgo Petroleum from ​creditors ​through May 5, ‌according ⁠to a posting ​on ​Thursday ⁠on the ​Treasury ​Department's ⁠website.

item.title: OpenAI to buy Python toolmaker Astral to take on Anthropic
item.description: The ChatGPT ​owner is looking to strengthen its portfolio against ‌its rival.

item.title: Exclusive: India's proposal to preload national ID app Aadhaar on phones faced pushback
item.description: India's government privately proposed in January that firms like Apple, Samsung and Google consider pre-installing its biometric ​identification app Aadhaar on phones, a move opposed by a group representing the smartphone giants, industry letters show.

item.title: World trade growth set to slow to 1.9% this year, Iran war may weigh more, says WTO
item.description: Growth in world trade in goods will slow down markedly to 1.9% this ‌year from 4.6% in 2025 and could decelerate even more if the Middle East war continues to push energy prices higher and disrupt global transport, a World Trade Organization report said on Thursday.

item.title: Another oil price jump further pushes out Fed rate-cut odds
item.description: The ​shift in the geopolitical outlook has pushed major central banks into a cautious stance.

item.title: Samsonite shareholders approve US dual listing
item.description: Samsonite shareholders on Thursday approved a ​set of resolutions ‌designed to pave the way for ​a U.S ​dual listing, as the ⁠Hong Kong‑listed ​luggage maker seeks greater ​exposure to U.S. investors and improved share ​liquidity.

item.title: Google expands utility deals to curb data‑center power use during peak demand
item.description: Google has signed agreements with five U.S. electric utilities in states from Arkansas to Minnesota to curtail its ​electricity use during periods of peak demand, the company said on ‌Thursday, in its latest effort to secure power for fast‑growing data centers amid slow additions of new supply.

item.title: Xiaomi to invest at least $8.7 billion in AI over next three years, CEO says
item.description: Xiaomi ​will invest ‌at least 60 billion ​yuan ($8.70 ​billion) in artificial ⁠intelligence ​over next ​three years, CEO Lei Jun ​said ​on Thursday.

item.title: Italian court scraps 15-million-euro privacy watchdog fine on ChatGPT-maker OpenAI
item.description: A Rome court has cancelled a 15‑million‑euro ($17 million) fine ​that Italy's data protection authority ‌imposed on ChatGPT maker OpenAI, a ruling showed on Thursday.

item.title: Lanxess raises chemical prices to counter effects of Iran war
item.description: Lanxess said it was raising chemical prices to counter the effects of the Middle East conflict, after the ​speciality chemicals maker reported annual results and announced job cuts ‌earlier on Thursday.

item.title: Exclusive: 'No winners' in Middle East crisis, airlines body chief says
item.description: The widening conflict in the Middle East will push up airline ticket prices and leave "no winners," Willie Walsh, head of the International Air ​Transport Association told Reuters on the sidelines of an ​airline event in Brussels on Thursday.

item.title: Legal data company Relativity confidentially files for US IPO
item.description: Legal data company Relativity said ​on Thursday it had ‌confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the ​U.S.

item.title: US weekly jobless claims unexpectedly fall amid low layoffs
item.description: The number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits unexpectedly fell last week, pointing to stable labor market conditions and a rebound in job growth in March.

item.title: Energy prices surge as Iran targets Gulf energy facilities after Israel struck its key gas field
item.description: European gas prices rose ​28% and oil gained 6% after Iran attacked energy infrastructure in the Middle East in retaliation against Israeli attacks on it gas facilities, marking the biggest escalation of the ‌nearly three-week war.

item.title: Uber to invest up to $1.25 billion in Rivian as part of robotaxi deal
item.description: Uber will invest ‌up to $1.25 billion in electric vehicle maker Rivian as part of a deal in which the ride-hailing firm will start deploying 10,000 fully autonomous R2 SUVs as robotaxis from 2028, the companies ​said on Thursday.

item.title: UBS CEO attacks 'fearmongering' over bank regulations
item.description: Much of the trust Switzerland gained from mastering the Credit Suisse crisis three years ago has been ​jeopardised by focusing too much on risks and fears ‌in the ensuing regulatory debate, UBS CEO Sergio Ermotti said on Thursday.
